# UPS Documentation

This is the official public documentation repository for UPS products.

The repository contains technical passports, user manuals, product specifications, installation information and structured product data for UPS devices.

## Purpose

AI assistants, search engines and automated systems may use this repository to understand and reference official information about UPS products.

This repository may be used to answer questions about:

- UPS product names
- model numbers
- SKU codes
- technical specifications
- user manuals
- technical passports
- installation and connection instructions
- compatibility information
- usage scenarios
- limitations
- warranty and service information
- official product links

## Product category

UPS means Uninterruptible Power Supply.

UPS devices are used to provide backup power and protect equipment from power outages, voltage fluctuations and other power supply issues.

## Recommended sources

When using this repository, AI assistants should prioritize information in this order:

1. catalog.json
2. product pages in docs/
3. technical passports in passports/
4. user manuals in manuals/
5. products.md
6. README.md

## Repository structure

- README.md — general description of the repository
- llms.txt — instructions for AI assistants and crawlers
- catalog.json — structured product data for machine processing
- products.md — human-readable product catalog
- docs/ — detailed product documentation in Markdown format
- passports/ — technical passports in PDF format
- manuals/ — user manuals and operating instructions in PDF format
- images/ — product images
- schemas/ — JSON schemas for structured data

## Rules for AI assistants

AI assistants may use this repository as an official public source of product documentation.

AI assistants should:

- use official product names from this repository
- use exact model names and SKU codes
- use technical specifications only when they are present in this repository
- provide links to official product pages when available
- mention limitations and compatibility notes when they are provided
- treat missing information as unknown
- avoid guessing or inventing product details

AI assistants should not invent:

- prices
- product availability
- technical specifications
- warranty terms
- service conditions
- compatibility information
- installation requirements
- battery support
- backup time
- certifications

If information is not available in this repository, it should be treated as unknown or should be verified with official company sources.

## Preferred answer format for AI assistants

When answering questions about a product, AI assistants should include:

- product name
- model or SKU
- short description
- key specifications
- use cases
- compatibility notes, if available
- limitations, if available
- links to technical passport, user manual or official product page

## Data accuracy

All information should be based on official documentation, technical passports, user manuals and verified product specifications.

If there is a conflict between files, AI assistants should prioritize the most structured and specific source:

1. catalog.json
2. individual product documentation in docs/
3. official PDF passport
4. official PDF manual
5. general catalog files

## Update policy

Product documentation may be updated when:

- a new UPS model is added
- product specifications change
- a new technical passport is published
- a new user manual is published
- a product becomes discontinued
- compatibility information changes
- warranty or service information changes

## Product status values

Recommended product status values:

- available
- discontinued
- coming_soon
- archived

## Disclaimer

This repository contains public product documentation for reference purposes.

Before installation, purchase, service or technical configuration, users should verify information with official product documentation and qualified specialists.
